photonics.label: Photonics
photonics.subtitle: Optical Interconnects
photonics.tooltip: Light-based data transmission inside AI data centers. Copper cables cannot handle 100kW+ GPU rack bandwidth — optical interconnects replace them.

memory.label: AI Memory
memory.subtitle: HBM & DRAM
memory.tooltip: High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) is physically stacked on top of NVDA GPUs. Every H100/B200 chip needs it. More AI training = more HBM demand.

fabs.label: Fabs & Foundries
fabs.subtitle: Chip Manufacturing
fabs.tooltip: The factories that physically manufacture chips. NVDA designs GPUs but does not make them — TSMC and others do the actual fabrication.

neoclouds.label: Neoclouds
neoclouds.subtitle: GPU Cloud Providers
neoclouds.tooltip: New cloud companies built purely for AI GPU compute. They buy NVDA GPUs in bulk and rent compute time to AI startups and enterprises.

materials.label: Materials & Packaging
materials.subtitle: Substrates & Test
materials.tooltip: Specialty substrates, wafer materials, and laser tools needed to build and package chips. The often-overlooked picks-and-shovels of AI.

networking.label: Networking
networking.subtitle: AI Cluster Connectivity
networking.tooltip: InfiniBand and Ethernet fabric connecting thousands of GPUs into one coherent cluster. Without this, individual GPUs cannot work together at scale.

center.title: NVIDIA + Hyperscalers
center.subtitle: GPU Designer & Buyers (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META)
center.tooltip: NVIDIA designs the GPUs that power all AI training and inference. Every company in this map either supplies into NVDA chips, connects to them, or runs on top of them.

unsortedCategory.label: Unsorted
unsortedCategory.subtitle: Awaiting classification
unsortedCategory.tooltip: Auto-added tickers ingested from posts that have not been categorized yet — triage with review.py.
